title: Power Automate - GitHub Connector Patterns And Constraints

confirmed_facts.fact: Microsoft publishes an official GitHub connector for Power Automate listed as Standard tier (not Premium). It exposes a Get all issues of a repository action with parameters: owner, repository name, milestone, state, assignee, creator, mentioned, labels, sort, direction, since, per_page, and page.
The GitHub connector is throttled at 100 API calls per connection per 60 seconds.

GitHub connector authentication is a delegated GitHub connection managed by Power Automate. The exact OAuth scopes requested behind the connection setup are not published in the connector reference documentation.
The HTTP with Microsoft Entra ID connector is specifically for resources authenticated by Microsoft Entra ID and is NOT the correct connector for GitHub's native token-based API authentication.
Power Automate Data Operations available for reshaping GitHub issue JSON inside cloud flows: Parse JSON, Select, Filter array, Create HTML table, Create CSV table, Join, and Compose.
Power Automate outbound synchronous HTTP call timeout limit is 120 seconds per single request.

When no suitable connector is available, the HTTP connector is the next-best method for one-off Power Automate API integrations, but connector-based automation is preferred first per Microsoft planning guidance.

implementation_notes: Recommended default flow shape: manual/scheduled trigger -> GitHub Get all issues of a repository -> optional Parse JSON -> Filter array (exclude pull requests) -> Select (reshape fields) -> Create HTML table or structured array.
Connector authentication is a Power Automate GitHub connection owned by the connection reference, not an inline token in the flow body. Do not document or screenshot tokens inside action definitions.
Avoid specifying exact connector OAuth scope strings in runbooks since they are not published; describe auth as a delegated GitHub connection managed by the connector.

For advanced scenarios requiring exact Link header pagination or x-ratelimit-* header inspection, use a raw HTTP action instead of the connector — the connector does not reliably expose raw response headers.
For raw HTTP fallback: use Authorization: Bearer <token>, Accept: application/vnd.github+json, X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28, and a valid User-Agent header.
For raw HTTP PAT-based flows targeting private repos, prefer fine-grained PATs scoped to the target repository with Issues: read.
Large repositories (>100 issues) require explicit paging loops; loop over the page parameter until the response is empty.
caveats: Connector does not expose raw pagination (Link) or rate-limit (x-ratelimit-*) headers; raw HTTP fallback required for advanced header-driven pagination control.
Connector OAuth scopes are opaque — the connector reference does not publish the exact GitHub scopes it requests.
GitHub connector response may include pull requests in the issues list; use Filter array to exclude objects containing a pull_request key.
HTTP with Microsoft Entra ID is a wrong connector choice for GitHub — it is Entra-scoped only.
